songs.description: The 615 Hideaway Records is proud to announce its first album release for the new label by Bluegrass veteran Audie Blaylock And Redline. Originalist includes six originals, including the single “Love Is An Awful Thing” and instrumental “Huckleberry’s Hope” and six songs specially picked out by Audie and the band made popular by many legends like Jimmy Martin, The Stanley Brothers, The Delmore Brothers and more. Lead vocal by Audie Blaylock and harmony vocals by Reed Jones and Even Ward.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Audie Blaylock; Guitar: Audie Blaylock; Bass: Reed Jones; Banjo: Even Ward; Fiddle: Mason Wright

songs.description: Better Angels Fly Away, written by Redline band member Reed Jones and tells the story of a son reflecting on the invaluable life lessons his father taught him as he copes with his disorienting loss. It explores themes like the nature of change, the loss that accompanies it, and what is required of us if we want to hold onto the wisdom of the past to pave the way to a better future.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Audie Blaylock; Harmony Vocals: Audie Blaylock; Harmony Vocals: Reed Jones; Guitar: Audie Blaylock; Bass: Reed Jones; Banjo: Scott Vestal; Fiddle: Patrick McAvinue; Mandolin: Patrick McAvinue

songs.description: At their best, songs help us make sense of the world. Different Than Today not only honors the legacy of my grandparents and their journey from eastern Kentucky to southwestern Ohio, the act of writing and recording it has helped me find my way forward in the face of this frenzied world, all by maintaining a healthy and meaningful connection to my own history. - Reed Jones
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Audie Blaylock; Guitar: Audie Blaylock; Bass: Reed Jones; Banjo: Even Ward; Fiddle: Mason Wright; Mandolin: Patrick McAvinue

songs.description: “I wrote this song in the wake of what appeared to be a posthumous Audie sighting. It was like he was reminding me that he was still right there with me. And I know he is. This song was written for me, my Redline brothers, and everyone who has experienced the pain of losing a loved one. Each of those groups gets a part of this song, and I hear Audie all over it. - Reed Jones
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Reed Jones; Harmony Vocals: Darren Nicholson; Banjo: Russ Carson; Bass: Reed Jones; Guitar: (Audie’s) Reed Jones; Fiddle: Patrick McAvinue; Mandolin: Patrick McAvinue; Banjo: Evan Ward; Fiddle: Mason Wright; Snare: Harry Stinson

songs.description: This Red Rover is definitely not child's play. Written by Audie Blaylock and Redline bassist and vocalist, Reed Jones, and inspired by a story his papaw told him of the "good ol' days" in eastern Kentucky, this latest Redline contribution demonstrates how some folks just can't leave the childish games behind. What starts as innocent fun as kids carries through to adulthood, when sometimes relationships can feel like childhood deja vu.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Audie Blaylock; Guitar: Audie Blaylock; Bass: Reed Jones; Banjo: Scott Vestal; Mandolin: Patrick McAvinue; Fiddle: Patrick McAvinue

songs.description: "Ophelia," originally recorded by The Band in 1975, is a song about a narrator's bewilderment and heartache after a woman suddenly vanishes from his life. Penned by Robbie Robertson, it explores themes of mystery, abandonment, and lingering, devoted love. Robertson revealed the name was inspired by the birth name of the country comedian Minnie Pearl (Sarah Ophelia Colley).
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Billy Emerson; Harmony Vocals: Teri Chism; Harmony Vocals: Wayne Lanham; Banjo: Bill Emerson; Guitar: Tom Adams; Bass: Teri Chism; Mandolin: Wayne Lanham; Fiddle: Shelby Gold

songs.description: David Parmley’s new album is aptly titled, So, What’d I Miss? on The 615 Hideaway Records. Parmley hit the ground running with two back-to-back #1 hit singles “All Dressed Up” and “West Virginia” on the Bluegrass Today Weekly Airplay Chart. In addition, four more charted hit singles were released and all six are currently bluegrass radio favorites including “Goodbye To The Blues, “What Makes You Think”, “I’ll Be here In The Morning”, “If You Can’t Feel It”.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: David Parmley; Harmony Vocals: Don Rigsby; Harmony Vocals: David Parmley; Banjo: Scott Vestal; Dobro: Jeff Partin; Fiddle: Tim Crouch; Guitar: Jim Hurst; Mandolin: Seth Taylor; Bass: Dave Roe

songs.description: “I’ve been thinking about doing a Cardinals tribute album for a long time and when the Bluegrass Cardinals were inducted into the IBMA Hall of Fame last year I thought this would be the perfect time to do the tribute album especially since it’s the 50th Anniversary of the Cardinals debut album released in 1976. Right from the beginning the Cardinals always wanted to have their own sound identity, even if we did a traditional song, we wanted to make it our own with the Cardinals’ style and sound. The tribute album, Cardinal Tradition (Now & Then) certainly accomplishes this on all of the new original and fan favorite songs” – David Parmley
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: David Parmley; Harmony Vocals: Larry Stephenson; Harmony Vocals: Doug Bartlett; Banjo: Scott Vestal; Fiddle: Tim Crouch; Dobro: Randy Kohrs; Mandolin: Jacob Burleson; Bass: Evan Winsor; Guitar: David Parmley

songs.songTitle: I'll Be Here In The Morning
songs.description: The 615 Hideaway Records is proud to announce the new single “I’ll Be Here In The Morning” is from Bluegrass top male vocalist and fan favorite David Parmley. “I’ll Be Here In The Morning” follows Parmely’s two previous Hit Singles “All Dressed Up” and “West Virginia” which both went #1 on the Bluegrass Today Weekly Airplay Chart. Written by Townes Van Zandt “I’ll Be Here In The Morning” conveys everyone to leave their worries behind. A powerful ballad that will remind everybody, no matter what you’re going through, that there is a sense of peace that will prevail in our hearts and minds. The song is a genuine expression of devotion and commitment, and promise of unwavering presence in the morning, regardless of what life may bring. “When I first heard Don Williams’s version of this song, I fell in love with it. I thought it would be great done acoustically. Hope the fans love it as much as I do!”. – David Parmley
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: David Parmley; Harmony Vocals: Don Rigsby; Banjo: Scott Vestal; Guitar: Jim Hurst; Fiddle: Tim Crouch; Bass: Dave Roe; Mandolin: Seth Taylor; Dobro: Jeff Partin

songs.description: David Parmley felt a tribute album would be appropriate to commemorate the 50 Year Anniversary of Bluegrass Cardinals’ Debut self-titled album released on March 23, 1976. So, 50 years later to the exact date David Parmley is releasing Cardinal Tradition (Now & Then) tribute album on The 615 Hideaway Records. “I’ve been thinking about doing a Cardinals tribute album for a long time and when the Bluegrass Cardinals were inducted into the IBMA Hall of Fame last year I thought this would be the perfect time to do the tribute album especially since it’s the 50th Anniversary of the Cardinals debut album released in 1976. Right from the beginning the Cardinals always wanted to have their own sound identity, even if we did a traditional song, we wanted to make it our own with the Cardinals’ style and sound. The tribute album, Cardinal Tradition (Now & Then) certainly accomplishes this on all of the new original and fan favorite songs” – David Parmley The ten-song tribute album contains five new original songs written by today’s top Bluegrass songwriters and performed in the Cardinal Tradition style. “No Empty Chairs In Heaven” already released from the album written by Rick Lang and Jack Shannon went #1 on the Bluegrass Today Gospel Weekly Airplay Chart. Just released is another new original song titled “Turning Acres Into Rows” written by Mark “Brink” Brinkman and David Stewart. The tribute album also includes fan favorite songs performed in the same Cardinal tradition including “You Took All The Ramblin’ Out Of Me”, “Rolling Away”, Mountain Laurel”, “Wonder Where You Are Tonight” and “Back Away”. Cardinal Tradition (Now & Then) is produced by David Parmley and Scott Vestal and was recorded at Digital Underground Recording Studio, Greenbrier, TN. Performances on the album include David Parmley, Del McCoury, Larry Stevenson, Don Rigsby, Becky Issacs Bowman, Sonya Issacs, Rob McCoury, Ron McCoury, Jason Carter, Tim Crouch, Scott Vestal, Rob Ickes, Cody Kilby and many more.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: David Parmley; Harmony Vocals: Larry Stephenson; Guitar: David Parmley; Banjo: Scott Vestal; Tim Crouch – Fiddle; Dobro: Randy Kohrs; Mandolin: Jacob Burleson; Bass: Evan Winsor

songs.description: 615 Hideaway Records is proud to announce the new single “West Virginia” from David Parmley, the follow up single to his #1 hit single “All Dressed Up” on 615 Hideaway Records. “West Virginia” written by Linda Buell and Jody Emerson tells the story of a man who has traded suits and slicker shoes for cowboy boots and a Stetson hat to follow his dreams of being a cowboy.

songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: David Parmley; Harmony Vocals: Becky Issacs Bowman; Harmony Vocals: Sonya Issacs; Banjo: Scott Vestal; Fiddle: Tim Crouch; Mandolin: Ronnie McCoury; Dobro: Rob Ickes; Guitar: Cody Kilby; Bass: Evan Winsor

songs.songTitle: You Took All The Ramblin' Out Of Me
songs.description: “I’ve been thinking about doing a Cardinals tribute album for a long time and when the Bluegrass Cardinals were inducted into the IBMA Hall of Fame last year I thought this would be the perfect time to do the tribute album especially since it’s the 50th Anniversary of the Cardinals debut album released in 1976. Right from the beginning the Cardinals always wanted to have their own sound identity, even if we did a traditional song, we wanted to make it our own with the Cardinals’ style and sound. The tribute album, Cardinal Tradition (Now & Then) certainly accomplishes this on all of the new original and fan favorite songs” – David Parmley
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: David Parmley; Harmony Vocals: Don Rigsby; Banjo: Scott Vestal; Fiddle: Tim Crouch; Dobro: Randy Kohrs; Mandolin: Jacob Burleson; Bass: Evan Winsor; Drums: Mike Rogers; Guitar: David Parmley

songs.description: Produced by Steve Wilson and Kristi Stanley, Kristi is the perfect showcase for Kristi Stanley’s beautiful mountain soul voice and is supported by some of the top Bluegrass musicians today including Andrew Crawford on guitar, Blake Young on mandolin, Steve Wilson on banjo, Glen Crain resonator guitar, Deanie Richardson on fiddle, Michael Branch on acoustic bass), Adam Plott on percussion, Aaron Bowen on keyboards, Shawn Lane, Gena Britt and Sarah Logan on harmony vocals.

songs.songTitle: Did You Tell Her All About Me
songs.description: Produced by Steve Wilson and Kristi Stanley, Kristi is the perfect showcase for Kristi Stanley’s beautiful mountain soul voice and is supported by some of the top Bluegrass musicians today including Andrew Crawford on guitar, Blake Young on mandolin, Steve Wilson on banjo, Glen Crain resonator guitar, Deanie Richardson on fiddle, Michael Branch on acoustic bass), Adam Plott on percussion, Aaron Bowen on keyboards, Shawn Lane, Gena Britt and Sarah Logan on harmony vocals.

songs.description: This new project embodies all that I love when it comes to music. It’s an eclectic mix that ranges from old school bluegrass to traditional, classic country sounds, with a little bit of soulful folk and rock. As you listen, you’ll catch a glimpse of my roots growing up in Eastern Kentucky and hear a snippet of what my music collection sounded like. It’s very special to me because it’s a reminder of where I started out with music as a young girl and an opportunity for me to truly unveil myself as an artist to the world.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Kristi Stanley

songs.description: Inside the Lines by Western North Carolina’s Nick Chandler & Delivered is a hard-driving, traditional bluegrass album. Released on 615 Hideaway Records, the project blends tight, four-piece vocal harmonies with exceptional picking, featuring a lineup of primarily original songs penned by Chandler himself.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Nick Chandler; Mandolin: Nick Chandler; Banjo: Spencer Hatcher; Guitar: Hudson Bosworth; Bass: Gary Trivette

songs.description: "Where Do We Go From Here" is On the Trail's first full length studio album. It features an eclectic mix of each band member's songwriting. The album takes the listener on a journey from traditional bluegrass to genre bending folk/pop inspired grooves and maintains a high level of instrumental prowess and vocal power.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Charlie Widmer; Harmony Vocals: Austin Scelzo; Guitar: Charlie Widmer; Mandolin: Tom Polizzi; Fiddle: Austin Scelzo; Bass: Matt Curley

songs.description: Unfinished Bridges, their 3rd project overall and second with The 615 Hideaway Record label, Rock Hearts recorded this with Scott Vestal at his Digital Underground studio in Tennessee. Many of the thirteen songs are originals by Brink Brinkman, Marc Gnezda, Rick Lang, Ted DeMille, Bob Amos, Jim Karns, Monty McClanahan, Stan Keach, Sam Tidwell, Billy Thibodeau, and Alex MacLeod. The project was engineered and mastered by Scott Vestal, who produced the album along with the members of Rock Hearts.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Alex MacLeod; Guitar: Alex MacLeod; Fiddle: Austin Scelzo; Banjo: Joe Deetz; Mandolin: Billy Thibodeau; Bass: Rick Brodsky
songs.songwriter: Charles Lewis Seitz, J. William Denny, Joe Cannonball Lewis

songs.description: This album, “Wanderlust” has it all! Hard-driving banjo licks, heartfelt fiddle melodies, dynamic mandolin playing, stunning harmony vocals, and superb arrangements in a fine selection of songs. All with a rock solid rhythm and bass foundation. If all that wasn't good enough, we get some guest appearances by none other than, Michael Cleveland on the title track 'Wanderlust'. The great Dale Ann Bradley also adds vocals on 'Driving Nails'. And last but not least, Alex MacLeod is joined by Jonathan Edwards on his song, 'Don't Cry Blue'.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: N/A; Guitar: Alex MacLeod; Fiddle: Austin Scelzo; Banjo: Joe Deetz; Mandolin: Billy Thibodeau; Bass: Rick Brodsky

songs.description: “Pretty Paper” written by Willie Nelson this Holiday Country Classics tells the true story of a disabled street vendor, selling Holiday wrapping paper, pencils and other items on the streets of Ft. Worth Texas. Peddling his wares, the man is hoping that holiday shoppers wouldn't pass him by. The message in the song tugs on the heart strings, while recognizing the significance and spirit of the holidays and all that they mean to us. Produced by Jonathan Edwards and recorded at Acadia Recording Company in downtown Portland, Maine.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Alex MacLeod; Harmony Vocals: Joe Deetz; Harmony Vocals: Billy Thibodeau; Guitar: Alex MacLeod; Fiddle: Austin Scelzo; Banjo: Joe Deetz; Mandolin: Billy Thibodeau; Bass: Rick Brodsky; Piano: Tom Snow

songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Alex MacLeod; Guitar: Alex MacLeod; Fiddle: Austin Scelzo; Banjo: Joe Deetz; Mandolin: Billy Thibodeau; Bass: Rick Brodsky

songs.description: This album, “Wanderlust” has it all! Hard-driving banjo licks, heartfelt fiddle melodies, dynamic mandolin playing, stunning harmony vocals, and superb arrangements in a fine selection of songs. All with a rock solid rhythm and bass foundation. If all that wasn't good enough, we get some guest appearances by none other than, Michael Cleveland on the title track 'Wanderlust'. The great Dale Ann Bradley also adds vocals on 'Driving Nails'. And last but not least, Alex MacLeod is joined by Jonathan Edwards on his song, 'Don't Cry Blue'.

songs.description: The brothers comprising the eastern Kentucky-born band are anything but newcomers: Jonathan & William Goodwin began their musical journeys playing together in the early 90s, Jonathan was 8 and William 6. Prior to that they spent time singing separately for the most part in church, school plays and events. It wasn't long thereafter the young talents released their first studio album (1999). Jonathan was 14, William 12. They began singing on the bluegrass festival circuit across the eastern US, a tenure that had refined their sound to a level typically reserved for older players with bigger discographies.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Will Goodwin; Harmony Vocals: Kenneth Bush; Harmony Vocals: Jonathan Goodwin; Mandolin: Will Goodwin; Banjo: Jonathan Goodwin; Bass: Mark Fain; Fiddle: Andy Leftwich; Guitar: Cody Kilby

songs.songTitle: I Feel The Blues Movin' In
songs.description: The song "I Feel the Blues Movin' In" is originally a bluegrass hit written by Del McCoury and famously performed by The Del McCoury Band. It has also been notably covered by sibling groups like The Goodwin Brothers and country/bluegrass artist William Lee Golden's group, William Lee Golden and The Goldens.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Will Goodwin; Harmony Vocals: Kenneth Bush; Banjo: Jonathan Goodwin; Mandolin: Will Goodwin; Bass: Mark Fain; Fiddle: Andy Leftwich; Guitar: Cody Kilby

songs.description: "Still the One" by the American soft rock band Orleans is a 1976 musical triumph. Written by John and Johanna Hall, this breezy track broke the mold by celebrating enduring long-term love rather than heartbreak.
songs.bandMembers: Lead Vocals: Will Goodwin; Harmony Vocals: Kenneth Bush; Mandolin: Will Goodwin; Banjo: Jonathan Goodwin; Bass: Mark Fain; Fiddle: Andy Leftwich; Guitar: Cody Kilby
